title: Ice on Mars: A Frosty Discovery
story: Frozen water on Mars? You bet! In 2002, the Mars Odyssey spacecraft made an exciting find. As it zoomed around the Red Planet on May 26, its instruments detected something cool - literally. Large deposits of ice were hiding just beneath the Martian surface. This discovery got scientists buzzing about the possibility of future human missions to Mars. After all, water is a key ingredient for life as we know it!
activity_question: What did the Mars Odyssey spacecraft discover on Mars?
follow_up_question: Why is finding water on Mars important for future space exploration?

title: Capturing the Sun's Disappearing Act
story: The sun vanished, and two brothers were ready! In 1854, William and Frederick Langenheim made history on May 26. They snapped eight photos in a row of a total solar eclipse. This was the first time anyone had photographed such an event in North America since cameras were invented. Their quick thinking preserved a rare celestial show for future generations to see.

title: The Beatles Paint the Charts Blue
story: Fab Four fever struck again! The Beatles' blue album, 'Beatles 1967-1970,' rocketed to number one in 1973. This collection of hits from their later years had fans twisting and shouting with joy. On May 26, the album proved the band's magic was still alive, even after they had split up. It was a colorful reminder of their incredible musical journey.
activity_question: What color was associated with the Beatles album that reached number one?
bonus_fact: The album is often called 'The Blue Album' due to its cover color.

title: Ellis Island: A Tale of Two States
story: Surprise! Ellis Island, where millions of immigrants first stepped onto American soil, isn't just in New York. In 1998, the Supreme Court settled a long-standing dispute, ruling that most of Ellis Island actually belongs to New Jersey. This decision on May 26 redrew the map, leaving only the original island in New York's territory.
activity_question: Which state did the Supreme Court rule owns most of Ellis Island?
follow_up_question: Why do you think Ellis Island's ownership was important to both New York and New Jersey?

title: Napoleon's Italian Crown
story: Imagine a powerful leader adding another crown to his collection! That's exactly what Napoleon Bonaparte did in 1805. On May 26, he became the King of Italy in a grand ceremony. Napoleon, already the Emperor of France, placed the ancient Iron Crown of Lombardy on his own head, declaring, 'God gave it to me, beware whoever touches it.'
activity_question: What did Napoleon place on his own head during the ceremony?
bonus_fact: The Iron Crown of Lombardy is over 1,000 years old!
